title: Portfolio Reports - Trick using Sub-items
excerpt:
Hello, 
we are already using Monday.com for project & portfolio management, but it is a struggle that Monday.com doesn’t have a status report function that is more than just different graphs.  We want to have data in Mon…

posts.author: Jmergen (Jessica Mergen)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,

we are already using Monday.com for project & portfolio management, but it is a struggle that Monday.com doesn’t have a status report function that is more than just different graphs. We want to have data in Monday.com that we can pull into reports to help drive adoption. Graphs just don’t tell the whole story.

We have a reporting format that is used in a fortnightly PMO meeting that we wanted to replicate. I thought I had hit on a way to do this using sub-items of projects in a portfolio, and text field columns that would be for function owners to populate their report data. And then I would pull that into a table in a Workdoc using the live connect function. Then once each PMO meeting is past I could create a PDF of the Workdoc, and save that as history. I would perhaps also create an automation to save the content of the text field updates as sub-item updates for history on the sub-item. So that way when the next report time came it would be OK to have the text fields over written.

Example of the sub items in the Portfolio with columns of desired data:

image1483×787 112 KB

And Example PPT format I was trying to replicate in a Workdoc using a table and live data connections to the above noted sub-items.

image1303×240 11.9 KB

And then I found out that Sub-items do not have as much functionality as primary items. You cannot interact with them in Workdocs with the live data connection. And I am back to square one. We could keep doing this just in a PPT, but I was really hoping to drive more people to Monday.com and showcase it as part of these regular PMO meetings.

Is anyone aware of how I could either do this with the API, or an app or integration that would do this - allow me to fully interact with sub-items so I can use them to gather written status reports for presentation in a meeting. Thanks!

posts.author: johannesgeyer (Johannes Geyer)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Jmergen ,

You can try the app PDF Report Generator to create a PDF in your own design, including information from items and subitems.

The design is fully customizable with the integrated report builder or with Microsoft Word (docx) templates.

You can also include a Subitem table in your report.

If you have any questions or need help with the setup, feel free to reach out to us: Contact form

Here’s a quick video tutorial on how to set it up:

posts.author: Jmergen (Jessica Mergen)

posts.author: Rissa1 (Rissa Ream)
posts.cookedHtml:
Could you enter the updates in a different board and link the board to your portfolio board? Your project board is already linked, but you could have another board linked where these updates go and use a connected boards view on the item card in your portfolio board to see the same information.

My example is I have a portfolio risk register as well as a change log. I can summarize the risks & changes on the project level and click in for more details and/or I can add the connected boards view to my item card to see the specific items and even edit from there. This gets you out of the limitations of sub-items and makes it possible to create your monthly doc PDF without having to wait on app approvals.

Also important to note I have workflows that create groups on the portfolio boards and connect the items automatically that make this possible.

Hope this is useful and if you would like any additional details let me know and I am happy to provide them.

Jessica Mergen:

Is anyone aware of how I could either do this with the API, or an app or integration that would do this

You can create fully customizable reports with the Analytics & Reports app by Screenful. You can create lists, tables, and charts using any of your columns from top level items or subitems.

CleanShot 2025-07-06 at 14.29.051920×1071 72.8 KB

Here’s a guide for creating reports and a guide for getting started with Screenful.

posts.author: Atul (Atul | cloudvandana apps)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Jmergen

You can use our app Build My Docs - monday.com marketplace which enables you to generate PPTX/PDF from Item Data onto a PPTX template.

You can copy Columns, Images, and subitem tables onto the PPTX.

The data copied over will follow the font style, design, and positioning of the original PPTX.

Here’s the example template you may use → PPTX Template

Make sure to replace the “Merge Values“ with the exact values for each column, subitem table from the settings section of our app, which is unique to each board.

If you need a demo, feel free to reach out → apps[at]cloudvandana.com

posts.author: Mariia (Mariia Mykhasiuk)
posts.cookedHtml:
That’s a common limitation teams hit with monday.com when they move beyond dashboards and need structured, repeatable PMO-style reporting, not just visual charts.

One option worth looking at is Time in Status for monday.com

It adds reportable, historical data (how long projects stay in each status, delays, bottlenecks, trends over time) that can be pulled into WorkDocs or exported, which complements text-based status updates really well. This way, even if sub-item text fields are overwritten each reporting cycle, the status history remains intact and usable for audits or comparisons.

Combining narrative updates in sub-items with objective time-in-status metrics tends to work much better for fortnightly PMO reviews than graphs alone.
